title: Proof of Cantor's Theorem via Diagonalization
description: A visual demonstration of Cantor's Theorem, which states that for any set X, its power set P(X) has a strictly greater cardinality. The diagram illustrates the core logic of the diagonalization argument used to show that no function from a set to its power set can be surjective.
elements: Subset D = { x ∈ X | x ∉ f(x) }
Contradiction (D not in range of f)
relationships: Assume f is a function mapping each element in X to a subset in P(X)
Construct a special subset D defined by elements not contained in their image under f
Show that D cannot be equal to f(y) for any y in X, proving f is not surjective
